heat_sources.rule: Count every heat source individually. Extract full specification from equipment schedule. Flag if specification is incomplete.

gas_boiler.name: Gas Boiler
gas_boiler.function: Primary heat generation — burns natural gas to heat water
gas_boiler.cascade_note: Multiple boilers in cascade = count each boiler + cascade controller (1nr) + common header + hydraulic separator. Each boiler has its own flue, gas connection, and isolation valves.
flag_if: Flue route not shown on drawings

electric_boiler.name: Electric Boiler
electric_boiler.function: Heat generation using electrical resistance elements — no gas, no flue
electric_boiler.note: No flue or gas connection needed. Requires heavy electrical supply — check electrical capacity.

heat_pump.name: Heat Pump (ASHP / GSHP / WSHP)
heat_pump.function: Extracts heat from air, ground, or water source — high efficiency heating (and often cooling)

ashp.name: Air Source Heat Pump (ASHP)
ashp.location: External — roof, ground level, or plant compound
ashp.notes: Needs clear airflow around unit. Noise to neighbours may be an issue. Defrost cycle in winter.

gshp.name: Ground Source Heat Pump (GSHP)
gshp.location: Internal plant room. Ground loop array external (boreholes or horizontal trenches).
gshp.notes: Higher COP than ASHP but requires ground works. Ground loop is a significant additional cost.

wshp.name: Water Source Heat Pump (WSHP)
wshp.location: Internal. Water source: river, lake, aquifer, or closed-loop ground water.
wshp.notes: Requires abstraction licence if open-loop. Heat rejection to water source.

associated_items: Buffer vessel (often required for heat pumps)
Ground loop array (GSHP only — measured in m of borehole or m of trench)
flag_if: Flow temperature not confirmed (affects radiator sizing)

chp.name: CHP Unit (Combined Heat and Power)
chp.function: Generates electricity AND heat simultaneously from gas. Electrical output offsets grid import.
chp.note: CHP is complex — involves both mechanical AND electrical scope. Cross-reference KB-E for electrical generation interface.
flag_if: Electrical and thermal outputs not both stated

district_hiu.name: District Heating HIU (Heat Interface Unit)
district_hiu.function: Connects building to district heating network — plate heat exchanger between primary (district) and secondary (building) circuits
district_hiu.note: HIU replaces boiler plant. Primary side is typically by the district heating company. Secondary side is the M&E contractor scope.
flag_if: Primary connection responsibility not clarified (often by others)

hot_water.rule: Count every HW generation unit. Note storage capacity (litres) and heating capacity (kW). L8 compliance affects storage temperature (60°C min) and distribution temperature (50°C at outlets).

calorifier.name: Calorifier / Indirect Cylinder
calorifier.function: Stores hot water heated by an indirect coil (LTHW or steam)
specification_required: Connections (primary in/out, secondary flow/return, cold feed, drain, T&P relief)
flag_if: L8 compliance not addressed in spec

direct_electric.name: Direct Electric Water Heater
direct_electric.function: Heats water directly using immersion elements — no primary heating circuit needed
direct_electric.note: Common for point-of-use applications and where no central heating is available. Requires dedicated electrical circuit — cross-reference KB-E.

instantaneous.name: Instantaneous Water Heater
instantaneous.function: Heats water on demand with no storage — gas or electric

types.gas: Gas-fired continuous flow (e.g. Rinnai, Bosch) — high flow rate, requires flue and gas connection.
types.electric: Electric instantaneous — typically low flow rate (hand wash only), high kW for flow rate provided.

specification_required: Flow rate (l/min at stated temperature rise)

thermal_store.name: Thermal Store
thermal_store.function: Stores heat (not hot water) — primary water in vessel, DHW heated by passing through coil in store
thermal_store.note: Different from a calorifier — in a thermal store, the stored water is the primary (heating) water, not the domestic hot water. DHW is heated instantaneously by passing cold mains through a coil.

cooling.rule: Count every cooling plant item. Note capacity (kW cooling). Cooling plant has significant electrical load — cross-reference KB-E.

chiller.name: Chiller
chiller.function: Produces chilled water (typically 6°C flow, 12°C return) for air conditioning

air_cooled.name: Air-Cooled Chiller
air_cooled.location: External — roof or ground level plant compound
air_cooled.notes: Self-contained with integral condenser fans. No cooling tower needed. Lower efficiency than water-cooled. Noise consideration for neighbours.

water_cooled.name: Water-Cooled Chiller
water_cooled.location: Internal plant room. Requires separate cooling tower or dry cooler externally.
water_cooled.notes: Higher efficiency than air-cooled. Requires condenser water circuit (additional pumps, pipework, cooling tower). More complex system.

associated_items: Condenser water pumps and pipework (water-cooled only)

cooling_tower.name: Cooling Tower
cooling_tower.function: Rejects heat from water-cooled chiller condenser circuit to atmosphere via evaporation
cooling_tower.note: L8 ACOP applies to open cooling towers — requires water treatment, monitoring, and risk assessment. This is a LEGAL REQUIREMENT.
flag_if: L8 water treatment not addressed in spec

dry_cooler.name: Dry Cooler / Adiabatic Cooler
dry_cooler.function: Rejects heat to atmosphere without evaporation (closed circuit) — alternative to cooling tower
dry_cooler.note: No L8 Legionella risk (closed circuit). Lower efficiency than cooling tower but simpler and lower maintenance.

dx_split.name: DX Split System
dx_split.function: Direct expansion cooling — refrigerant-based, typically for individual rooms or server rooms
dx_split.note: Simpler than VRF — typically 1 outdoor to 1 indoor (or small multi-split: 1 outdoor to 2-5 indoor). For VRF/VRV systems see KB-M03.
flag_if: Indoor and outdoor units not both shown

distribution.rule: Count every distribution item. Pumps are critical — note duty point (flow rate and head). Always check for duty/standby configuration.

pump_single.name: Pump (Single)
pump_single.function: Circulates water through heating, cooling, or domestic hot water circuits

types.inline: Inline (glandless/canned rotor) — compact, pipe-mounted. Common ≤DN80. Lower maintenance.
types.base_mounted: Base-mounted (end-suction) — floor-mounted on concrete plinth. For larger duties. Requires foundation.
types.close_coupled: Close-coupled — motor directly coupled to pump. Compact base-mounted option.

pump_set.name: Pump Set (Duty/Standby)
pump_set.function: Two pumps — one operates (duty), one on standby for redundancy or peak load

configurations.duty_standby: One pump runs, other on standby. Auto-changeover on failure or timer.
configurations.duty_assist: One pump runs normally, second starts for peak demand.
configurations.duty_duty: Both pumps run simultaneously, each sized for 50% of total duty.

pump_set.count_rule: Count as 1nr SET (not 2nr pumps). Note configuration (D/S, D/A, D/D). Note individual pump duty.
pump_set.associated_items: Same as single pump × 2, PLUS: common header/manifold, changeover controls, non-return valves on each discharge.

expansion_vessel.name: Expansion Vessel
expansion_vessel.function: Absorbs thermal expansion of water in closed heating/cooling circuits — maintains system pressure
expansion_vessel.count_rule: Count every vessel. Note capacity. One per closed circuit typically.
expansion_vessel.note: Expansion vessel is a MANDATORY item for every sealed/pressurised system. If not shown on drawings but sealed system exists, flag as potentially missing.

pressurisation_unit.name: Pressurisation Unit / Pressurisation Set
pressurisation_unit.function: Maintains system pressure automatically — combines expansion vessel, pressure switches, top-up valve, and controls
pressurisation_unit.note: Replaces simple expansion vessel + filling loop for larger commercial systems. Includes integral pressure gauges, safety valve, and low-pressure alarm.

buffer_vessel.name: Buffer Vessel / Thermal Buffer
buffer_vessel.function: Provides thermal mass to prevent short-cycling of heat pumps, boilers, or chillers. Smooths load fluctuations.
buffer_vessel.note: Particularly important for heat pump systems — prevents compressor short-cycling. Also used for chiller systems with variable flow.
flag_if: Heat pump system shown without buffer vessel — flag as potentially missing

plate_heat_exchanger.name: Plate Heat Exchanger (PHE)
plate_heat_exchanger.function: Transfers heat between two fluid circuits without mixing them

types.brazed: Brazed plate — compact, non-demountable. Standard for small-medium duties.
types.gasket: Gasketed plate — demountable for cleaning. Standard for larger duties and where fouling is expected.

low_loss_header.name: Low Loss Header / Hydraulic Separator
low_loss_header.function: Decouples primary (boiler/heat pump) circuit from secondary (distribution) circuit. Allows independent flow rates.
specification_required: Connections (primary in/out, secondary in/out, drain, air vent)
low_loss_header.note: Required for multi-boiler/multi-pump systems. Allows primary and secondary circuits to have different flow rates.
flag_if: Multiple boilers/heat pumps shown without hydraulic separation — flag as potentially missing

water_treatment.rule: Count every water treatment item. Often specified in commissioning section of spec rather than on drawings. Check both.

chemical_dosing.name: Chemical Dosing Pot / Feeder
chemical_dosing.function: Introduces corrosion inhibitor and biocide into closed heating/cooling systems
chemical_dosing.note: BSRIA BG 29 requires chemical treatment for all closed water systems. If not shown on drawings, check spec — it may be specified by clause rather than drawn.
flag_if: Closed system (LTHW/ChW) without chemical dosing — flag as potentially missing

water_softener.name: Water Softener
water_softener.function: Removes calcium and magnesium from make-up water to prevent scale in boilers, calorifiers, and cooling systems
water_softener.note: Required in hard water areas. Check local water hardness. Essential for boiler and calorifier longevity.
flag_if: Hard water area but no softener shown — flag for spec check

uv_steriliser.name: UV Steriliser
uv_steriliser.function: Ultraviolet light treatment for potable water — kills bacteria without chemicals
uv_steriliser.note: Sometimes required for rainwater harvesting systems or borehole water. Check spec.

filtration.name: Filtration Unit
filtration.function: Removes particulates from water — various types for different applications

types.strainer: Y-strainer or basket strainer — coarse filtration at point of entry.
types.cartridge: Cartridge filter — finer filtration. Replaceable cartridge element.
types.sand: Sand filter — for swimming pools, cooling tower make-up.
types.carbon: Activated carbon filter — for taste/odour removal, chlorine removal.

filtration.count_rule: Count every filter unit. Note type and micron rating if specified.

side_stream.name: Side-Stream Filtration Unit
side_stream.function: Continuously filters a portion of circulating water to remove suspended solids — extends system life
specification_required: Flow rate (% of system volume per hour)
side_stream.note: Increasingly specified on commercial LTHW and ChW systems. Improves heat transfer efficiency and extends equipment life.

gas.rule: Count every gas item individually. Gas installations require Gas Safe registered installers. Flag if gas scope boundary (utility vs M&E) is unclear.

gas_meter.name: Gas Meter
gas_meter.function: Measures gas consumption — interface between utility supply and building installation
gas_meter.count_rule: Count if shown. Usually supplied and installed by gas utility company (by others).
gas_meter.flag_reason: Gas meter is typically a UTILITY CONNECTION — confirm if by M&E contractor or by gas utility. Coordination with utility company required. Lead time may be significant.
gas_meter.note: M&E scope typically starts DOWNSTREAM of the gas meter. Upstream is utility company scope. Confirm boundary.

gas_governor.name: Gas Governor / Pressure Regulator
gas_governor.function: Reduces gas pressure from mains pressure to building operating pressure
gas_governor.note: Often supplied with the gas meter by the utility company. Check scope boundary.

gas_solenoid.name: Gas Solenoid Valve
gas_solenoid.function: Electrically operated gas shut-off valve — closes on fire alarm signal or gas detection alarm
gas_solenoid.note: Gas solenoid valves are SAFETY items. Require electrical connection to fire alarm or gas detection panel — cross-reference KB-E.

gas_unit_heater.name: Gas-Fired Unit Heater
gas_unit_heater.function: Direct gas-fired space heater — warehouse, workshop, loading bay heating

types.warm_air: Warm air unit heater — fan blows air over gas-fired heat exchanger. Ceiling-suspended.
types.radiant: Radiant tube heater — infrared radiant heating. Ceiling-mounted. No air movement.
types.plaque: Radiant plaque heater — ceramic element. Intense localised heat.

gas_pipework.name: Gas Pipework (Internal Distribution)
gas_pipework.measurement: m (linear metres). Material: carbon steel (black steel) or copper depending on size and application.
gas_pipework.note: Gas pipework must be installed by Gas Safe registered engineers. Requires pressure testing (BS 6891). Check spec for purging and testing requirements.

priority: 1. EQUIPMENT SCHEDULE: If provided, this is the AUTHORITATIVE source. Use it for count, specification, and model selection.
2. GA DRAWING: Count equipment from drawings if no schedule exists. Extract labels and annotations for specification.
3. SPECIFICATION CLAUSES: Spec may describe equipment requirements without a schedule. Extract key parameters.
4. NEVER ESTIMATE: Do not invent equipment specifications or quantities. If information is missing, flag it.
extraction_rules: Extract model number if shown on schedule or drawings.
Extract all performance data: capacity (kW), flow rate (l/s), pressure (kPa), electrical supply.
Note manufacturer name if specified (and whether "or approved equal" is stated).
Cross-reference schedule quantities with drawing locations — flag any discrepancies.
Equipment without ANY specification data should be flagged as "Low confidence — spec required".
schedule_rules.associated_items_rule: Every major equipment item has ASSOCIATED ITEMS (valves, flexibles, strainers, connections, controls). Check the associated items list for each equipment type and include them in the takeoff. These are often not individually drawn but are always required.
flag_triggers: Equipment shown on drawing but not in schedule — flag: "On drawing but not scheduled"
Equipment in schedule but not visible on drawing — flag: "Scheduled but not located on drawing"
Equipment without capacity/specification — flag: "Specification incomplete — Low confidence"
Equipment with "TBC" or "TBA" annotation — flag: "Specification to be confirmed"
Utility connections (gas meter, electrical intake) — flag: "Confirm scope boundary — may be by others"
